description: Test-level routing handlers (PR-A: continue / stop via the routed-spec sequencer). Only PASS/SKIPPED permutations are expressible as a fixture (the combined suite asserts 0 fails): a test that PASSes with onPass continue:true (an explicit no-op that resolves PASS), and a test that PASSes with onPass stop:spec, which stops the spec's remaining tests so they resolve SKIPPED. The FAIL-path behaviors (FAIL stop:spec, the FAIL->stop:test no-op default) are asserted in focused it() tests in core-core.test.js, since they resolve to FAIL. Cross-platform via `node -e`.

tests.description: A passing test routes onPass continue:true. continue is the PASS default, so this is an explicit no-op: the test resolves PASS and the next test runs normally.

steps.description: Passes; the test's onPass continue:true is a no-op and the next test still runs.

tests.description: A passing test routes onPass stop:spec. The test itself resolves PASS, but the spec's remaining tests are stopped and recorded SKIPPED.

steps.description: Passes, then stops the spec — the following test is skipped.

tests.description: Reached only after the prior test stopped the spec, so this test is SKIPPED (never executed). Its step would PASS if it ran.

steps.description: Never runs — the prior test stopped the spec.
